title: Sonchus bupleuroides
intro: Sonchus bupleuroides, also known as double-stemmed sowthistle or Bupleurum-style Sowthistle, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress and modulate the immune system. Bioactive constituents of Sonchus bupleuroides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sonchus bupleuroides is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Sonchus bupleuroides is a species of thistle in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and other parts of Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The species is classified as a biennial or perennial plant, producing yellow flowers in clusters. Sonchus bupleuroides is further classified within the genus Sonchus, which contains several other species of thistles and sowers. Within the plant kingdom, Sonchus bupleuroides is classified as a eudicot, belonging to the clade Rosidae.
